entry.title: How to connect an HTTP OnPremise destination using BasicAuthentication to a Fiori App in BAS on SAP?
entry.content:
I am working on SAP BTP and need to connect a destination to a Fiori App in the Business Application Studio. Here are the relevant details:

  1. Destination Details:
    - Type: HTTP
    - Proxy Type: OnPremise
    - Authentication: BasicAuthentication
  2. Cloud Connector:
    - Version: 2.14.2
    - Java Version: 1.8.0_252 (IcedTea)
  3. Exposed Back-End Systems:
    - Protocol: HTTP
    - Back-End Type: ABAP System

How can I connect the above destination to a Fiori App on SAP Business Application Studio?

entry.title: Re: Ship to Party address :SAP EWM
entry.content:
I have similar requirement to retrieve ship to party BP document address country but we need it in BADI to overwrite staging area. I have found badi but I am not able to find the address country as reading the in-flight EWM outbound delivery order gives address number which is not yet created.

Require Help!

entry.title: Re: Loading data to Planning enabled Model through Analyt...
entry.content:
Hi JefB 

Thank you so much looking in to this issue:

Which I can edit and publish via story, unblock the selection input

 

Story after publish the data - See simple Table_1.getDataSource().getResultSet()

 

Simple Account dimension Model with 3 dimension Account, Project & Time.

Same intersession of data

  • Same Account
  • Same Project
  • Same Time
  • SignedData change to different.

getPlanning().setUserInput – also very simple

 

  

Expected the submitData  should change rawValue of the account 94000 à 45000

But always getting the error :

 

Any lead could help to fix this script. Really appreciated talking time to looks deep into this issue.

entry.title: Re: TIME_OUT in t-code VA02 while deleting document
entry.content:
Line 105 of the DELETE_TEXT function module.

LOOP AT CATALOG WHERE TDOBJECT = OBJECT 
  AND TDNAME IN R_NAME 
  AND TDID IN R_ID 
  AND TDSPRAS IN R_LANGUAGE.

The CATALOG table has one more item per iteration (not the LOOPs one, but each time this FM is called within the process, what appears to be infinitelly -does this word exists?-).

I've found the CATALOG contains some text items that are not in the document, but related (like parterns' texts or so). I cannot understand the logic that puts those items in the deletion queue... just happy that the process crashes before it deletes all those texts.

entry.title: The Carrot Catastrophe
entry.content:
I was once working with a client in the frozen foods industry, tasked with integrating their SAP S/4HANA system with Warehouse Management System (WMS). The project was in its final stages, and just before the go-live date, we were rigorously testing various scenarios to ensure everything would run smoothly. Little did I know, a seemingly minor error in my Groovy script would lead to a comedy of errors.

During the testing phase, we began sending delivery IDocs from SAP. The process seemed straightforward, and I focused primarily on the technical aspects, paying close attention to field names and unit codes. However, in my meticulous concentration on the code, I neglected to verify the actual data being transmitted.

To my utter disbelief, I soon received a phone call from a client representative. The normally calm voice was now tinged with frustration and incredulity. The reason? The company had inexplicably received an order for several thousand tons of carrots. Yes, carrots. Apparently, due to a misconfiguration in my script, the system had interpreted some field values incorrectly, resulting in an astronomical order of this humble vegetable.

The most amusing (and slightly mortifying) part of the story was that this erroneous order, worth only a few euros, had triggered the dispatch of dozens of trucks filled to the brim with carrots. Imagine this scenario is productive system, people standing before a veritable sea of orange, and dozens of trucks lined, ready to deliver an absurdly large shipment of carrots.

In the end, the situation was resolved without too much trouble. The errant script was corrected, the delivery orders were recalibrated, and the carrots were redirected. While the incident certainly caused a stir, it also provided a much-needed laugh in the otherwise serious world of software integration.

This experience taught me the invaluable lesson of double-checking data outputs, no matter how technical the process might be.

And of course, it left me with a funny little story that I’ll never forget - the day I accidentally ordered a mountain of carrots.

entry.title: SAP HCM: S4 HANA: CAT2 Entry: Remove Warning message for Weekends like Saturday and Sunday
entry.content:
Wanted to remove warning message while entering time entry for Saturday and Sunday in CAT2 transaction.

to remove warning message is 

Attendance/absence 15.06.2024 - 15.06.2024 during non-working period (att./abs.type A175)

Message no. HRTIM00REC009

Please help

entry.title: Re: concatenate hrpad23 fields to validate with dbtablog...
entry.content:
Hello,

You can use Open SQL string functions without concatenation, that can suit your needs. For example

  SELECT
    h~mandt,
    h~adatanr
  FROM hrpad23 AS h
  INNER JOIN dbtablog AS d
  ON
     left( d~logkey, 3  ) = @SY-mandt AND
     h~adatanr = substring( d~logkey, 4, 10 )
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_temp).

Another possibility is to use Native SQL, AMDP'S or ADBC so you can execute a more complicated query with HANA Native SQL.
Lastly it is also possible to just fetch the data in internal tables and use logic in ABAP to keep the records you want.

entry.title: Re: Loading data to Planning enabled Model through Analyt...
entry.content: Hi JefB - Thank you for deep look at this issue. I have repost the case with input-ready cell and manually posted, same via script SetUserInput and submitData with error on debug window, Please check and let me know any missing part {<selection>}. not much reference {<selection>} format or field values.

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP: Change sections on Object page dynamically...
entry.content: Solved with: https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/blob/main/06_object_page_content.md#hiding-features

entry.title: Re: TIME_OUT in t-code VA02 while deleting document
entry.content:
From your description, it sounds as if you can at least replicate it easily enough. Have you worked with dynamic logpoints already which can be set up via TCode SDLP and can be created based on a breakpoint? If you put one at the problematic statement and try to capture potentially helpful field content in the logpoint you might be able to gain some more insights. It's for example possible to have it capture the call stack and other helpful "entities". The online description for SDLP is quite helpful.

Here is the code as I see it in our NW 7.50 EHP8 system with SP28:

*     ...Texte sofort loeschen..........................................
 WHEN SAVEMODE_DIALOG.
      DELETE FROM  STXH CLIENT SPECIFIED
       WHERE MANDT    =  CLIENT
         AND TDOBJECT =  OBJECT
         AND TDNAME   IN R_NAME
         AND TDID     IN R_ID
         AND TDSPRAS  IN R_LANGUAGE.
      DELETE_COUNT = SY-DBCNT.
      DELETE FROM  STXB CLIENT SPECIFIED
       WHERE RELID    =  'TX'
         AND MANDT    =  CLIENT
         AND TDOBJECT =  OBJECT
         AND TDNAME   IN R_NAME
         AND TDID     IN R_ID
         AND TDSPRAS  IN R_LANGUAGE.
      DELETE FROM  STXL CLIENT SPECIFIED
       WHERE RELID    =  'TX'
         AND MANDT    =  CLIENT
         AND TDOBJECT =  OBJECT
         AND TDNAME   IN R_NAME
         AND TDID     IN R_ID
         AND TDSPRAS  IN R_LANGUAGE.

*     ...Texte ueber Verbuchung loeschen................................
 WHEN SAVEMODE_POSTING.
      PERFORM IMPORT_CATALOG.
*     ...Aufnehmen Texte aus Datenbank in Textkatalog...................
      IF TEXTMEMORY_ONLY <> 'X'.                             "B20K015761
         SELECT * FROM STXH
          WHERE TDOBJECT =  OBJECT
            AND TDNAME   IN R_NAME
            AND TDID     IN R_ID
            AND TDSPRAS  IN R_LANGUAGE.
            PERFORM READ_CATALOG USING STXH-TDOBJECT
                                       STXH-TDNAME
                                       STXH-TDID
                                       STXH-TDSPRAS.
            IF SY-SUBRC > 0.
*              ...Text noch nicht in Textkatalog........................
               ADD 1 TO DELETE_COUNT.
               CATALOG_CHANGED = TRUE.
               PERFORM INSERT_CATALOG USING STXH-TDOBJECT
                                            STXH-TDNAME
                                            STXH-TDID
                                            STXH-TDSPRAS
                                            FUNCTION_DELETE
                                            FALSE.           "B20K074874
            ENDIF.
         ENDSELECT.
      ENDIF.                                                 "B20K015761
*     ...Texte in Textkatalog bearbeiten................................
      LOOP AT CATALOG WHERE TDOBJECT =  OBJECT
                        AND TDNAME   IN R_NAME
                        AND TDID     IN R_ID
                        AND TDSPRAS  IN R_LANGUAGE.
         IF TEXTMEMORY_ONLY = 'X'.                           "B20K015761
            DELETE CATALOG.                                  "B20K015761
            CATALOG_CHANGED = TRUE.                          "B20K015761
            ADD 1 TO DELETE_COUNT.                           "B20K015761
         ELSE.                                               "B20K015761
            CASE CATALOG-FUNCTION.

Hope that any of this helps!

Cheers

Bärbel

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
I am an SAP Business One Trainee Consultant from Sialkot, Pakistan. As a CA student and a commerce graduate, I am keen to expand my skills and knowledge in the SAP ecosystem. I am particularly interested in learning SAP Analytics Cloud and believe that the digital skills initiative by SAP Learning is a fantastic opportunity for individuals like myself. I am eager to take on the challenge and undergo the necessary training to achieve SAP Certification.

Thank you for considering my interest in this initiative.

entry.title: Re: Is STEM Education the Future of Schooling?
entry.content: In the context of learning, it is impossible to avoid mention of STEM – Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics – as these subjects are most popular due to their high relevance to real life and the consequent economic value, as well as the ability to address global issues. I remember a time when I did not know a thing about technology and English teaching, and getting into this area was quite scary. When I first joined the school, I had my fair share of doubt regarding the applicability of STEM for many of the students and wanted to incorporate a meaningful STEM lesson into the classroom without causing harm to my students’ learning. In the same manner, I have a reliable option of a cheap educational service to assist me with cheap law assignment.

entry.title: Re: Link between OJDT+JDT1 Join OPCH+PCH1 without repeating rows
entry.content: could you mark the correct reply? or post it again? thank you.

entry.title: "Uncaught TypeError" during "EnrichFinance" Step in "Business Partner Creation Approval Process"
entry.content:
We have imported and testing "Business Partner Creation Approval Process" live process project from SAP Build Process Automation Store.

We are able to reach until "EnrichFinance" step without any issues.

But on "EnrichFinance" step, when we're trying to add Bank or Tax Data in My Inbox task, nothing happens.

There are no calls being made to any URL.

But in Console, we see errors when we are clicking on "+" button

For Bank Data -

For Tax Data -

Upon navigation to the referenced Component-preload.js, we see below line of code in failed state -

onAddTaxNumber: function() {
                        var e = this.getParentModel().getProperty("/BPRequest/FinanceData/TaxData");
                        e.push({
                            TaxCategory: "",
                            TaxNumber: ""
                        });
                        this.getParentModel().setProperty("/BPRequest/FinanceData/TaxData", e);
                        this.getParentModel().refresh()
                    },

Trigger to the process -

We are triggering process via API. Below is our payload -

{
  "RequestId": "1718213572",
  "BPRequest": {
    "BusinessPartnerCategory": "2",
    "BusinessPartnerName": "Bhupesh LLC",
    "RolesData": [
      {
        "BusinessPartnerRole": "Z001"
      }
    ],
    "AddressData": {
      "Country": "IN",
      "PostalCode": "400101",
      "CityName": "Mumbai"
    }
  },
  "RequesterDetails": {
    "RequesterUserId": "P000001",
    "RequesterFirstName": "Bhupesh",
    "RequesterLastName": "Yadav",
    "RequesterEmail": "<hidden>"
  },
  "internal": {
    "isRework": false
  }
}

 Please let us know if we are missing anything.

entry.title: Re: Running Your Own Blockchain on The SAP BTP Kyma Trial: A Hands On How To Guide 🚀
entry.content:
for anybody interested in SAP and Blockchain for Enterprise, here are the other blogs in this series:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/why-i-love-sap-and-blockchain-databases-and-why-you-should-too/ba-p/13625869

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-enterprise-architecture-positioning-blockchain-database-as-an/ba-p/13629842

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-enterprise-architecture-let-the-use-case-find-the-blockchain/ba-p/13632458

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/oil-amp-gas-ultimate-data-security-blockchain-data-backbone-from-ot-to-sap/ba-p/13640699

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/bcp-business-continuity-planning-for-sap-s-4hana-made-easy-with-enterprise/ba-p/13647824

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/trustable-ai-thanks-to-sap-ai-core-amp-sap-hana-cloud-amp-sap-s-4hana-amp/ba-p/13662822

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/b2b-business-processes-ultimate-cyber-data-security-with-blockchain-and-sap/ba-p/13680992

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/ringfencing-amp-decoupling-s-4hana-with-enterprise-blockchain-and-sap-btp/ba-p/13639467

Thank you!

Dmitry

entry.title: VSCode Fiori Adaptation Project Issue - SAP S/4HANA 1909 On-premise
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a problem with the adaptation project for these two applications:

  • Manage Journal Entries https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F0717')/S28OP
  • Display G/L Account Balances https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F0707')/S15OP

Request: Add custom columns within the smart table.

S/4HANA Version: SAP S/4HANA 1909

Fiori Launchpad Version: 1.71.21

I created an adaptation project for the first application using Visual Studio Code. When I open the SAPUI5 Visual Editor, I get this message: "Have in mind that synchronous views are detected for this application and controller extensions are not supported for such views. Controller extension functionality on these views will be disabled."

I am unable to perform custom logic through the "extend with controller" option because it is disabled:

Regarding the second app, I am unable to create the adaptation project at all:

What do you suggest?

the latest blog:

Running Your Own Blockchain on The SAP BTP Kyma Trial: A Hands On How To Guide 🚀

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA 1909 - VSCode Fiori Adaptation Project - Manage Journal Entries (F0717)
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a problem with the adaptation project for this application:

  • Manage Journal Entries https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F0717')/S28OP

Request: Add custom columns within the smart table.

S/4HANA Version: SAP S/4HANA 1909

Fiori Launchpad Version: 1.71.21

I created an adaptation project using Visual Studio Code. When I open the SAPUI5 Visual Editor, I get this message: "Have in mind that synchronous views are detected for this application and controller extensions are not supported for such views. Controller extension functionality on these views will be disabled."

I am unable to perform custom logic through the "extend with controller" option because it is disabled:

What do you suggest?

entry.title: Re: Merge almost identical process variants in Process Co...
entry.content:
Hi Nils, 

the tricky thing is that "almost identical" is very subjective and context-dependent. For some events, repeating them twice is almost identical. However, for paying an invoice, executing the step twice would be horrible. Regardless of the philosophical challenge of "almost identical" being subjective and context-dependent: No, it is currently not possible to merge similar variants. 

We will soon allow hiding and grouping events (not just visually but in actual Signal calculations), meaning that some noisy events could be removed. In addition, we will record your feedback to further investigate the topic of variant grouping. 

Best
Manuel from SAP Signavio

entry.title: Initialize delta flag for a Delta DTP in BW Bridge
entry.content:
Good day Community

Is there an option in BW Bridge to initialize the delta flag on a DTP with a delta load setting? There is an option in BW to initialize the flag on the info package. I cannot seem to find a similar option in BW Bridge especially since info packages are no longer supported. 
I searched for a similar option in BW Bridge but only found the delta initialization without data. 

Is the only option to load a full load and then initialize the flag by selecting the "delta initialization without data"?

Thank you

Regards
Sulaiman

entry.title: MDK - Asset Manager - build - ERROR node:crypto
entry.content:
I am building Asset Manager for Android.

  • MDK: 23.8.7-002
  • SAM: 2310.0.3
  • Node: 16.14.0
  • Webpack: 5.92.0

I have built SAM many times before. The project is in a virtual machine with Windows, VM is used only for building this version of SAM. Today I needed to recreate client (use command create-client.cmd). Command failed with error:

ERROR in node:crypto
Module build failed: UnhandledSchemeError: Reading from "node:crypto" is not handled by plugins (Unhandled scheme).
Webpack supports "data:" and "file:" URIs by default.
You may need an additional plugin to handle "node:" URIs.

Some dependencies are installed each time when the create-client.cmd is executed. Maybe some dependencies have changed? I am not aware of any changes in the VM. I have tried MDK 24.X, but same error. Build in BTP Mobile Services does not have this problem.

Request: Add custom columns within the smart table.

S/4HANA Version: SAP S/4HANA 1909

Fiori Launchpad Version: 1.71.21

I am unable to create the adaptation project at all, and this message is shown:

What can I do?

entry.title: How to make infotypes mandatory for po13?
entry.content:
Dear SAP community members,

I have been wondering if it is possible to make certain infotypes mandatory for creating a new position (po13). I would appreciate any suggestions and references.

entry.title: Re: Analysis for Office - Logon was successful - Loading...
entry.content: Hi @Henry_Banks unfortunately, we were not able to solve this problem 100%. What helped us was to set the preferred platform and to empty the caches via the technical configuration. We noticed that this misbehavior occurs when the connection is briefly interrupted during login. We then restart Excel and it usually works.

entry.title: Re: Schedule a background job to execute a Transactions
entry.content:
Hello @athakyriaz ,

You suggest me to make this from SM36 Transaction ?


 Yes you should use SM36.

There is a business requirement to manual create legacy asset using tcode AS91 in SAP ECC, but as you can see below snapshot "Takeover values" field is greyedout. This requirement is for ECC environment, please suggest us what to be done?

Regards,
Chandan

When running Exchange Rate Differences, one of the accounts includes transactions without FC conversion. While another account excludes the without FC conversion. 

Can you please advise why this is happening?

Thank you.

How to know the requester Dept regardless creating P.R document type for each Dept or Cost Centre Number ,  creating purchase group for the Depts ?

entry.title: Re: How can I get the model data of the Object Page in my extension in a Fiori Elements app?
entry.content: perfect solution,thanks Mahesh.

entry.title: Re: Denied Party Block Function 66
entry.content:
In standard you can 'block' FI vendor via FD05 at company code level or global.

Else you could add activate a custom FI validation via GGB0 with a user-exit for any code required.

entry.title: Re: TM in SAP S/4HANA 2023: Maintain Carbon Emission on Vehicle Type
entry.content:
Hello Michael, All, 

I did spent time over the realise notes for 2023 and tested the CO2 setup in our S4 instance embedded TM. I make all work as described on FO.

But only thing i did not fully understood the usage / reason of the tab "CO2 Emission" at Freight Unit level (under product). 

It does not calculate anything and maybe it should not even, as in FU I dont have resource (i.e. truck nor truck type yet) and the stage has no KM calculated yet. 

What is the purpose of these 2 fields? Is that for the corner scenario that some customers would bind their FU to resource already before planning? Or? 

Thank you for the advice.

Cheers

Jakub

entry.title: Re: SFSF Employment Details Alert is only triggered after a change in the portlet
entry.content: It depends on how the job is scheduled in provisioning system, if it have been scheduled

entry.title: Re: RAP: Side Effect for child entity table
entry.content:
Hi,

Can you share the content of ls_reported? It should contain the info about which entity is failing and for what reason. Please tell more about this error of yours.

BR, Serhii.

entry.title: Re: Schedule a background job to execute a Transactions
entry.content: Usually schedule the report behind the transaction, RAPOST2000, with SM36.

entry.content: Can I build the P.R release strategy based on requisitioner field in P.R?

thanks for sharing this insight. 

Regards!

entry.title: Re: MDK - Asset Manager - build - ERROR node:crypto
entry.content: Please check SAP Note # 3480260

entry.title: SAP Green Token Launched as Generally Available!
entry.content:
On behalf of the entire SAP Sustainability team, we are pleased to announce that the sustainability solution SAP Green Token is now Generally Available (GA) as part of the Digital Supply Chain (DSC) portfolio.

What does that mean? Well, it means that the SAP Green Token solution is now available as a standard solution to SAP customers and partners. With the high SAP standards being fully baked into the core solution.

 

If this is your first time hearing about SAP Green Token, say no more! Welcome to SAP Green Token 101. 😉

SAP Green Token is a software-as-a-service cloud solution that supports material traceability and transparency with segregation or mass balancing, tokenization, and a blockchain-based chain of custody for trust, improved efficiency, and compliance with recognized standards and regulations.

In short… SAP Green Token enables companies to proof that the materials they source, and sell are truly certified sustainable. ✔️

Key capabilities include:

  • Scalable mass balance and segregated accounting and reporting to ensure continuous compliance with evolving standards and regulations such as EUDR, RSPO and ISCC 🌴📦🛒
  • Holistic declaration management for various schemes and scopes
  • Rapid adoption at scale for all types of organizations through integration with SAP and non-SAP systems, and CSV exports. 💻

Learn more about the solution on the SAP Green Token Product Page, Help Page, and Roadmap Explorer. Cannot wait to get hands-on? Do you have any other questions? Feel free to reach out directly.

 

The ones familiar with the solution may know that Green Token has been around for a few years. The incubation of Green Token has been a journey of a lifetime. Let’s briefly reflect on a few core milestones.

  • 2019: the concept behind SAP Green Token wins ‘1 Billion Lives’: SAP’s flagship social intrapreneurship program that aims to improve 1 billion lives through a portfolio impact ventures.
  • April 2020: SAP Green Token joins SAP.io as an internal venture. The goal? To proof the concept at scale and go from innovation to activation, to transformation. And so we did in the years to come…
  • 2020-2022: the team worked with over 50+ industry leaders and organizations to validate, improve and innovate on the solution. A special thank you to our early innovators such as Unilever, DIC Chemicals, and our Chemicals & Packaging pilot groups.
  • September 2022: SAP Green Token transitions from SAP.io to SAP Sustainability.
  • 2023-2024: Achieved the first go-lives and enabled +150 experts world-wide from SAP partners and employees across regions. Including Accenture, Capgemini, Deloitte, KPMG and Osh Pro.
  • June 2024: Officially launched the solution as Generally Available

Going forward, the team will continue to release new features and functionalities, increase the list of standard use-cases, and further ramp up the SAP eco-system.

Thank you to our customers, partners, colleagues, and the core SAP Green Token team that have been and continue to contribute to SAP Green Token’s success! 👏 💚

And a special thank you to Andrew Mehes, James Veale, Mathias Held, and Nitin Jain for starting this journey over 4 years ago and leading the way while always remaining positive, empowering, humble, and inspiring.

 

📢  Stay tuned for more because more exciting things are coming soon!

 

*************************************************************************************************

Hi! 👋 I’m Gloria Figaroa. Since 2015, I live and breathe sustainability and supply chain management. 🍃 I’ve had the pleasure to work on Green Token for the past 4+ years as a Product Manager. Now in my latest role as Product Marketing Manager, I take care of everything Go-to-Market. 🌍

Follow me on SAP Community to stay up to date on the SAP Green Token solution, its newest features and functions, and detailed overviews of its capabilities. You will hear it first here!

entry.title: Re: Create a custom section fragment with event handler i...

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Integration Suite - New Message Metrics
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
Hello @Florian_Kube 
I found this post on the SAP Cusomer Influence.

We at the Deutsche Bahn would also be interested in publishing the API. It would be nice if you could upvote the suggestion.
But you could submit a separate feature request again, then I would like to upvote it, because the API is necessary for us.

Kind regards,
/Max

entry.title: Re: Jokes corner
entry.content:
Hi,

saw a funny short video:

Wizard and Barbarian approach a gate with 2 guards.

One guard telling the truth, one guard lying.

Wizard: 'I know that one. We have to...'

Barbarian pulls out axe and chops down one guard.

Wizard: WHATTT?

Barbarian grips remaining guard at neck and asks hinting to the slain guard: 'This guard dead??'

Guard hesitating: 'No?'

Barbarian smiling: 'This one lying!'

entry.title: Re: Schedule a background job to execute a Transactions
entry.content:
Hello @Romanos , 

in Project Field what you recommend to type ?

entry.title: Re: Using Job Scheduler in SAP BTP [14]: FAQs
entry.content:
I want to execute a job that runs from 8:00 UTC to 10:00 UTC, every 10 mins. I am using this cron pattern to do so but the job scheduler is scheduling jobs after 10:00 UTC also.

format: * * * * 8:12 */30 0

Any input helps

Thanks

entry.title: The code is getting assign in state standard field but not getting populate in ui
entry.content:
we have issue state & country values are passed As we checked the issue from PDI side, we can see that the state & country values are passed correctly from the PDI but the standard field (State) is not getting populated.As I checked further in the similar case It mentions by the dev is "BeforeSave/AfterModify do NOT retrieve the LeanLead object but the Lead object. Also, updates within PDI coding should happen only on the Lead object. Any access to LeanLead can lead to unexpected results."

Also as I check in the repository explorer, it mentions you need to create a new custom BO apparently but as we checked the example it dont not mention on how do we retrieve the Region_Code.Requesting  help us understand why is the field not getting populated

Regards 

Ramesh

entry.title: TAS order issue
entry.content:
Hi there

We have a TAS order issue.  A TAS order was set up and a PO generated.  When the invoice came through from the supplier it was for 20kg more than the original sales order.  The purchasing team goods receipted the full quantity but when the dispatch was done the original sales order quantity was delivered and invoiced.  VA05 now keeps showing 20kg open on the sales order.  

Any idea of what we can do to fix this?  We cannot reverse the goods receipt as our year end has closed.

 

Monika

entry.title: Re: Stuck in userlane tutorial. Can I skip the step?
entry.content:
Hi @Margit_Wagner 

Thank you for your answers. I now found how to get to the tutorial with the error: I get there when I start a free trial here and then just follow the first tutorial that appears.

The same mistake still happens as I described above and it is quite at the beginning and I can't skip past it.

Best regards,
Fausto

entry.title: Re: SFSF Employment Details Alert is only triggered after a change in the portlet
entry.content: It depends on how the job is scheduled in provisioning system. if the job parameters have been selected as Last successful EC alert job Run date, then it would only pick those employee records where the data have been modified -1 day (Yesterday) and compare its attributes with the rule conditions, if meet EC alert will get triggered. Further based on Alert effective date the triggered Alert will stay in backend (Manage alert and notification) And, if in the job "specify a date" have been selected in job parameters (recommended for one time run only), then the job will pick each employee records effective given date and validate it with the alert rule conditions and trigger alert.

entry.title: Fiori Attachment Service: DMS Documents
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

i am trying to use the attachment service in Fiori together with DMS within the Purchase Order (F0842A). Therefor I created a document type within “DC10” and now I am trying to find the object link which has to be attached to the Purchase Order. I think it should be either EKKO or EKPO but I am not sure about this. I tried using these object links but the attachments are still stored using GOS rather then DMS.

If anyone has any methods to troubleshoot the issue or how to find the correct object links, it would be highly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: BP customer not able to save as showing error as no e...
entry.content: Cross check this  SAP's  documentation

entry.title: Standard Scope vs Public Sector Scope: A Key Decision for SAP Public Sector Implementations
entry.content:
Whether it's your calling or career, as an implementation consultant, you’ll frequently find yourself untangling the complex web of ERP systems. 
This blog provides a deeper dive into the Standard Scope vs Public Sector Scope debate, to help you navigate the complex web of an ERP system within the Public Sector.


1. Key Choices

Understanding when to use Public Sector Management (PSM) instead of the Enterprise Management (EM) scope. The answer lies in identifying whether there are elements within a project that correspond with various facets associated with the public sector. For instance, if you’re dealing with multilateral reporting across different account assignments, consider the Public Sector Scope. This scope is also essential for year-end processes tied to government frameworks or specific governmental budgetary processes.
Below we have summarized and illustrated the key choices you have to make to decide on the correct Scope Version:


2. Public Sector Management Considerations 

Public Sector Management implementation also captures specific to fund accounting. This is instrumental for nonprofit governmental entities, especially in North America, that need to maintain checks and balances on the usage and distribution of resources. For instance, the reporting framework within PSM is multifaceted. It allows for data to be grouped, controlled, and analyzed across multiple dimensions, such as fund, grant, budget period, budget account, functional area, organization, or project. Additionally, other processes such as commitment carry-forward and residual budget processing, specific to government-related year-end procedures, are also part of PSM functionality.

Moreover, government-related budgetary processes need a unique approach. For instance, increasing the budget from revenue is a requirement for many government agencies. To enable proficient control and oversee these processes effectively, Public Sector Management is equipped with these functionalities, thereby eliminating obstacles and facilitating these specialized processes.

Furthermore, when dealing with grants, within PSM, SAP delivers special processes such as indirect cost and cost-sharing. In a public sector organization dealing with myriad grants, these processes become vital, impacting budgeting and resource allocation. Similarly, the PSM Scope utilizes period-based commitment updating to ensure the precise tracking of all commitments using accounting practices.

In regards of budget consumption and asset acquisition, the PSM Scope recognizes expenses, payments, and collections instead of costs and revenues. By configuring your system with the PSM  Scope, this functionality transitions the system from merely tracking depreciation to understanding and recording the consumption of budget during asset acquisition.

When managing costs and expenses, the PSM Scope offers a detailed perspective, differentiating between the two. In the corporate world, an expense usually equates to cost. However, in the public sector, an expense should be differentiated as a commitment that leads to future payment, while cost refers to the process that doesn't always result in a future money outflow (for example, depreciation). This differentiation provides a more detailed report, enabling better financial management within public sector organizations.

In terms of integrations, the PSM Scope is already integrated with SAP SuccessFactors for position budget control and with Concur for commitments. These integrations not only enhance the system’s capabilities but also simplify the overall operations of a public sector organization.


3. Evaluation 

No doubt using the PSM Scope comes with its own set of limitations. One limit to note when implementing the PSM Scope is the automatic activation of the baseline scope, which includes around 180 choosable scope items. These items are defined based on the typical needs of public sector organizations. Nonetheless, some scopes can appear to be a hard boundary for public sector needs, such as sales incentives, cost of sales, and so forth. It’s important to note that SAP will continue to further improve the Standard Scope items in the future based on market needs. 

Considering these complexities, it's recommended that consultants carefully evaluate their clients’ needs and evaluate whether the use of Public Sector Management is needed and beneficial. The takeaway is that a one-size-fits-all approach won’t suffice in understanding these scopes. Careful assessment and tailored solutions will be the key to unlocking the real potential of your ERP implementations for the public sector. In addition, partners may find the vast number of available scope items overwhelming, raising questions about which choices best correspond to the organization’s needs.



4. Conclusion

It’s essential to understand that each government organization will have unique requirements. For example, we had customers in certain countries/regions who categorized taxpayer accounting as part of ERP. Other government departments might have different definitions of ERP and accounting processes, which can involve the calculation and collection of taxes. In conclusion, it's important to remember that every institution will have unique requirements, and while the PSM Scope offers a wide range of functionalities, consultants should always prioritize their clients' needs. Applying a flexible approach, coupled with an in-depth understanding of the standard and public sector scopes, will truly enable consultants to extract the highest performance from their ERP implementations, ultimately benefitting the organization at large. Therefore, the Standard Scope vs Public Sector Scope isn’t a competition but rather a chance to maximize functionality based on each entity's special needs.


Co-Authored w/ Frank Godeby, Public Sector Product Management

entry.title: One Delivery and 2 Separate Sales Invoices - SAP Business One V10 FP2108 SQL
entry.content:
AdvanceOne Limited

Raju Parmar (raju@aoneafrica.com)

SAP Business One Version 10 FP 2108 – MSSQL 2017

We have a Government Requirement as follows:

One Delivery is made to a Government Agency of Item A 100 Units.

During Invoicing 2 separate Sales Invoices should be made as follows:

70% (70 Units) of the Invoice Value should go to theDistrict (Customer X) with PriceList 02 and

30% (30 Units) of Invoice Value should go to Government Agency (Customer Y) with PriceList 03.

Both the Sales Invoices A) and B) should reference to the same Delivery.

Has anyone come across such a scenario or have a work around.

Any assistance will be highly appreciated.

entry.title: IDOCs (INVOIC02) basic type
entry.content:
I have copied INVOIC02 and created ZINVOIC02. My question is: Are all user exits from INVOIC02 also automatically copied into ZINVOIC02 new custom type? 
OR do I need to make developments/implementations in order for that to work?

entry.title: Unleash the Energy! June Issue of SAP for Oil, Gas, and Energy Newsletter Now Available
entry.content:
I'm excited to announce the release of the June issue of the SAP for Oil, Gas, and Energy Newsletter!

This latest edition summarizes the unforgettable SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference, held this April in sunny Seville. It further introduces the recently released SAP Oil, Gas, and Energy Whitepaper 2024, which offers insights into the industry's future, especially with the integration of GenAI. Delve into success stories such as HPCL's transition to the SAP Business Technology Platform and OMV's next-level cybersecurity with SAP Enterprise Threat Detection. Also, don't miss the energy transition perspective from ExxonMobil and updates from our esteemed partners.
We hope you enjoy this issue with many insights, inspiration, and industry advancements.

Happy reading!

 

Subscribe here to receive the next issue directly in your e-mail box!

Felipe B.

entry.title: Producer content appears in Instant Producer
entry.content:
Hello community, 

A customised dictionary has been created for SEN.
The SME's were created to work in Instant Producer.
It was therefore necessary to set the customised dictionary as standard on each account and therefore have them open Producer. This meant that when Instant Producer was opened, all the content already in producer appeared in their Instant Producer.

How can I remove this for all SME?

Thanks in advance for your feedback!

entry.title: Re: issue with multiple discount determination at sales o...
entry.content: Probably, the related screen shot would help for better understanding.  How about in sale order condition tab screen ?  When you say, after credit release, new pricing is updated, did you check any custom validation is causing this issue ?  If at all, there is any incorrect configuration, this issue should occur for all sale orders but since you said the issue is only randomly happening, I feel, it could be due to custom validation.

entry.title: Re: Convert Json format date to SAP readable format
entry.content: Answered here... https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-discussions/time-conversion-from-unix-timestamp-epoch-seconds-since-19700101/td-p/3706257

entry.title: Re: SAP RDP connection to SAP ECC
entry.content:
Hi Michael,

SAP RDP Integration Accelerator is a package designed to connect SAP RDP with SAP ECC or/and SAP S/4 HANA systems. Here’s an overview of its features and how it works:

  1. Overview: The RDP Integration Accelerator provides an out-of-the-box ABAP integration package to quickly integrate data from backend SAP systems into the SAP RDP application. It includes predefined integration content that supports compliance with regulations like EPR and plastic tax.
  2. Key Features:
    1. Off-the-Shelf Integration: Ready to deploy and reduces development time compared to custom implementations.
    2. Compatibility: Supports SAP ECC 6 EhP7/EhP8 and S/4 HANA versions 1909, 2020, 2021.
    3. Prebuilt Content: Facilitates the selection, extraction, and transfer of necessary data.
  3. Benefits:
    1. Rapid Deployment: Setup can be completed in less than a day.
    2. Comprehensive Data Integration: Handles all necessary SAP data for RDP compliance.
    3. Cost-Effective: No additional licenses required and includes regular updates and support.

This package ensures efficient integration with minimal setup time, making it a preferred option over custom implementations or manual data import methods.

For further details on this, please contact Dilip Radhakrishnan (dilip.radhakrishnan@sap.com).

Thank you,

Dora

entry.title: Re: PaPm cloud Count number employees or other field
entry.content:
Hi Juan,

For this requirement I would suggest you try column pivot if you are in show screen and play around it.

I hope this helps!
Paulo M.

entry.title: RfcFunction.Invoke() is stuck when its session is destroyed
entry.content:
We manage sessions implementing SAP.Middleware.Connector.ISessionProvider. I'm trying to cancel a session invoking SessionChanged event when the RFC call takes too long.

 

ISessionProvider.SessionChanged.Invoke(new RfcSessionEventArgs(RfcSessionManager.EventType.DESTROYED, sessionId))

 

I'm testing this cancellation logic using RFC_PING_AND_WAIT on 2 systems:

  • System A: Release:789 / KernelRelease:753 / PartnerRelease:750
  • System B: Release:789 / KernelRelease:781 / PartnerRelease:755

On the System A, it works as expected. RfcCommunicationCanceledException is thrown from CpicConnection.CpicReceive() with a message "Connection to (xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx,3300) with conversationID [xxxxxxx] was canceled".

However, the cancellation doesn't work on the System B. Besides, RfcFunction.Invoke() doesn't finish even after the wait time of RFC_PING_AND_WAIT passes. According to trace logs, it is stuck after obtaining a lock.

 

2024-06-13 11:41:50.601 [Thread 9] CpicConnection.CpicCancel requested lock for instance 30880833 of Object: System.Object

 

How can I make it work on the System B as well? If it is not possible, is there a way to know whether the destination supports the cancellation?

* It occurs on NCo 3.1.3.0, 3.1.4.0 and 3.1.4.3. I have not checked other versions.

entry.title: Re: SAP ByD : extraction of Supplier invoices PDF
entry.content:

I have created a macOS app to do this - you can check it out at https://paulmcnally.azurewebsites.net/mac_os_supplier_invoice_attachment_extraction

Kind regards,

Paul

entry.title: Re: New video series for SAP Datasphere data marketplace
entry.content:
Thank you so much Florian for the detailed and informative demo videos which are explained quite in detail.Furthermore it would be also great if you can also cover how to configure Two SAP Datasphere tenants where one tenant act as Provider and another as Consumer so that seamless data replication can take place between two tenants. As per as I am aware there is a separate guide from SAP which explains whom to contact for the tenant configuration from SAP as stated below and most likely we need to contact Data Marketplace - Step-by-Step Guide for Data Pro... - SAP Community.

Is there any other sap help guidelines released which can make this process more easy and flexible in terms of integrating two SAP Datasphere tenants and where and how we can connect SAP for the assistance required for specific consumer and provider configuration.

Thank you so much.

Regards,

Hemanta

entry.title: Re: How to configure the web server to log the HTTP Heade...
entry.content: Unless you're using a non-default application server, this is really a Tomcat question.  See this page with some information on logging request/response headers.

entry.title: Create two or more quotations for 1 in-house repair object
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm configuring S4/HANA Service In-House Repair Process.

My client would like to have the process as follows:

  1. Create REPA with Objects
  2. Create Repair Quotation for Object X
  3. Create another Repair Quotation for Object X
  4. Process with Repair Order form the second quotation.

Is this possible and if yes, how do you configure this?

Today I have to process from quotation to the order. Also, when I reject the quotation, the process is done. I would like to have as many quotations as possible, and then continue to the order.

The business solution is very simple: when e.g. the car is broken, we create seperate quotation for the client, and separate quotation for the purpose of insurer with different pricing. I must have both in the system.

entry.title: Re: RfcFunction.Invoke() is stuck when its session is des...

Hey,

I believe your issue has nothing to do with Sap Universal ID. Would suggest correcting the tag so you get experts for your SAP solution looking at the issue.

Best,
Sebastian Schuck

entry.title: variant matching at lower BOM items
entry.content:
Is it possbile to replace lower BOM items with variant matching? 

 

e.g. Material A has a BOM consisting of B C and D configurable materials. All these explode  in a SO when A is configured.

when A is configured in a sales order, B is also copied with the same char values. For the Char values, B has a material variant B1. Is it possible to replace B with B1 in a sales order where A is configured?

entry.title: Re: [SAP BTP Onboarding Series] Joule – Getting Started with Joule and SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi @Kheren,

Employee central is not a per-req for Joule. However, please note that most of the functionalities are with EC. With the new releases of FAQ in the next few weeks, you will find Joule to be of good use without EC. So get started 😀.

entry.title: SDI Flowgraph Passing the Variable with Dynamic Value like Current Date
entry.content:
Hi All,

I need your help to resolve the issue of performance as the functions(like current_date) are not pushing down to the Source system. This is SAP SDI tool and adapter limitation. as a query. We are looking for alternate solution, like using flowgraph in SDI where we can define variables and use this in filter to load the data. Please find the flowgraph we have created for testing. During testing, we picked up the Virtual table as the source in the data source and added the filter section.

 

In the filter section, we defined the below where condition with a variable. We ran the task, and it ran successfully and quickly as we had sent the static values in the variable condition.

CDATE Variable has been defined as Variable in Properties section with Default value as Static date.

Now, we want to add a Variable to this task where the current date will be replaced by a Variable (CDATE).
So for that we created the Stored Procedure where we will define some variable(EX_DATE) and mapped this to CURRENT_DATE and we tried to call the task with below procedure.

After saving this when we tried calling the procedure and the procedure is working but is it not passing any current value in the task.


This is where we are stuck now. Could you help us with what needs to be done here?

We followed the below SAP blogs to implement this. We are very close to achieving this, but we are missing a few basics. Could you please shed some light on what needs to be done? Looks like we are not calling the way the task has to be called when we have the variable in flow diagram.

entry.title: Re: Exchange Rate Differences show incorrect amount
entry.content:
Hello, 

SAP KBA 3366138 .  This is a good starting point to try and understand what the system is doing and the implications of the account currency. 

Look at individual transactions and see if they make sense. If they still don't make sense, give us some more details about the transactions and maybe screenshots of the account currency and Journal Entries with all currencies displayed. 

We hope that helps,

Jesper

Do check our support blogs: https://blogs.sap.com/tag/b1-support-spotlight/

...and our Guided Answers.

entry.title: Re: How to kill a task in DWC ?
entry.content:
Hello,

How did you solve it? Do you have to enter the HANA Cockpit?

Greetings!

entry.title: Re: Can I build the P.R release strategy based on requisi...
entry.content:
Hi,

Yes, you can trigger the Purchase Requisition (PR) release strategy based on the requisitioner field in the PR. To achieve this, you need to maintain a Z-table for all the input fields related to the requisitioner. Additionally, provide a drop-down menu for the requisitioner field. The release strategy will be triggered based on the selected requisitioner. The respective users will be given authorizations only by the authorized requisitioner maintained in the role.

Note that there is no specific object for the transaction code ME51N. Therefore, we have implemented a custom object i.e. ZREQST, to manage this functionality.

Reference link here

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/pr-release-strategy-field/qaq-p/8474728

Regards

entry.title: Re: [SAP BTP Onboarding Series] Joule – Getting Started with Joule and SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi @dyasser,

Thank you, for the feedback. Happy to see that the blog is helping.

To answer your question, Joule will remain a single line item while you look at your entitlements. However, it can be used for multiple setup like Preview or production and it should work good. Same applies for Workzone - foundation edition which is always free. 

Hope this helps with your question.

Regards,

Nagesh

entry.title: Re: Change Key field in table
entry.content:
Hello @rajsapabap-tech ,

If you change the key fields, the table will need adjustment. See Adjustment of Database Structures .
This can lead to a loss of data, depending on the new table key and the availability of the table during the adjustment period. 

In your case you will probably not lose anything from the key change, since you are adding a key field, but the table will be unavailable to calling programms during the adjustment period and this can lead to runtime errors, loss of data etc. Meaning it can create problems somewhere else.

entry.title: Honey, I Shrunk the General Settings
entry.content:
If you wonder where the ‘General Settings’ disappeared in Web Client, this blog will tell you where to find it.

Starting from SAP Business One 10.0 Feature Pack (FP) 2405, the content of the ‘General Settings’ as you know it in the desktop client, is available in the Web Client, organized by the different modules.

This is part of a bigger restructuring of the Administration menu in Web Client that consolidates the settings and setup to be under the respective modules. The reason behind this change is that in desktop client, the configuration of a module is distributed across many forms in SAP Business One, some under General Settings, some under Company Details and so on - in Web Client, we’ve organized the configuration by SAP Business One modules (e.g., Inventory, Accounting, etc.) so you will find all related configurations in one place.

Terminology wise, Configuration = ‘Settings’ + ‘Setup’, where ‘Settings’ refer to values represented by checkbox, radio button and list of values, while ‘Setup’ refers to records of setup data, such as Item groups.

The configuration model in Web Client is illustrated as follows:

 

Sub-module is a logical split of the configuration module, related to a specific functionality.

The module and sub-modules are listed on the tree menu and the related Settings/Setup appear both as a menu entry (in the tree) and as the content itself in the main area across one or more tabs.

While in SAP Business One desktop client, Settings and Setup can be in the same window, in Web Client it is separated, i.e., setting and setup are in different menus.

This model is flexible and adjusted according to the module content – a module can have zero or more sub-modules/settings menus/setup menus, depends on the module nature and content richness.

Let’s see some examples – first example is for the ‘Payments and Banking’ module:

As can be seen, this module contains two sub-modules: Banking and Payments. Each contains only Setup records, such as Banks and Credit Cards. In this specific module there are no Settings menus.

 

A second example is the ‘Sales and Purchasing’ module:

This module contains no sub-modules; it contains three Settings menus and one Setup menu (Freights). The Settings menu is divided to several tabs on the main area for better usability. Many of the ‘General Settings’ items in desktop client that are related to documents are now available under this module.

 

Since some of the new settings menus in Web Client do not exist in desktop client, we’ve added new entries in the Authorization tree to support the authorizations for these forms.

The configuration menus are searchable (both settings and setup menus) and linkable – you can access the setup/settings page by using the ‘Define New’ option (e.g., from Business Partner master data, jump directly to Business Partner Customer Group to define a new setup record).

To conclude, the new configuration framework in Web Client consolidates all configuration elements under their respective module, offering easier orientation and navigation to configure your solution.

entry.title: Transition from SAP Enterprise Support reporting cockpit to SAP for Me Customer Insights dashboard
entry.content:
We are excited to announce that the the planned transition from the SAP Enterprise Support Reporting Cockpit to the new SAP for Me reporting has been completed and now the new interactive analytical  Customer Insights dashboard in SAP for Me is fully replacing the SAP ES reporting cockpit.

SAP ES reporting cockpit was originally created for the SAP ONE Support Launchpad and has being displayed embedded into SAP for Me starting from May 2023. At the same time we have been steadily developing the successor  - The 'Customer Insights' reporting dashboard' in SAP for Me, which has already been live since September 2023. The new Customer Insights dashboard offers far more upgraded features with a user-friendly interface, extended data, combined deployment approach, and enhanced performance. The development has been done  in close collaboration with customer user group and based on customers' feedback.

SAP Enterprise Support reporting cockpit will be sunset by the end of June 2024. 

Going forward, all updates, changes, and improvements will be incorporated solely into the Customer Insights dashboard, emphasizing its importance and long-term functionality. We highly encourage you to use and familiarize yourself with this new feature and provide us with your invaluable feedback. This will help us make further improvements to the reporting system designed for you.

To access the new dashboard, make sure your S-user possesses the 'Display Support Situation Reporting' authorization, which can be provided by your super administrator. For more details kindly refer to this KBA.

Please visit Customer Insights  portal page to stay up-to-date and get all information on access and authorization, latest updates, road map, trainings and info sessions, FAQs and more.

We hope, you will love the new reporting application Customer Insights Dashboard and enjoy using it to make the most of what it has to offer.

Thank you for your support during this transition.

Please use the ‘Feedback’ slider on the right edge of the page of the application to share with us your feedback on this new tool and help us to improve the reporting functionality in SAP for Me.

Stay updated by subscribing to this blog and visiting the SAP Support Portal / Customer Insights.

SAP for Me is made for you!

Related blogs:  all related blogs to ‘Customer Insights’ reporting dashboard,

entry.title: Re: Edge Integration Cell Setup with AWS
entry.content:
In the end we were able to solve the issue (not on AWS but on SUSE Rancher in a private landscape).

The issue was related to cluster configuration that wasn't fully compliant with longhorn requirements with SUSE Rancher. My suggestion is to double check the cluster configuration to verify if everything is fine.

entry.title: Re: BTP Integration Suite API Management and Stage Releva...
entry.content:
Hi Alex, 

For URLs and credentials you can utilize the Key Value Map capability of the APIM solution. Still you have to maintain it once like with configuration in Cloud Integration but after transport it will stay available. We do encrypt the credentials but URLs and other config we are just using without encryption. To make clear what something is in KVM we are using the API proxy name at least or a specific generic name and a prefix for Url or authentication type.

Hopefully this is giving you an insight of a possible option.

Kind regards, 

Jordy

entry.title: Re: Requester Dept For P.R
entry.content:
Hi,

Refer to this link

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/add-user-defined-filed-to-pr-item-screen-in-me51n-create-pr/qaq-p/5226141

Regards

entry.title: Re: Schedule a background job to execute a Transactions
entry.content: There are multiple guides on how to schedule a Background Job in SM36 including the link i posted above. You can test your solution with a z program.

entry.title: Depreciation not calculating for fixed assets
entry.content:
Hi,

Depreciation is not calculating for fixed assets for period 12 in previous fiscal year.

The capitilized date, depreciation start date is 01.03.2024. Acquisition date = 01.04.2024, Asset value date = 01.04.2024

These are assets that were uploaded through the migration cockpit as bulk uploads in 05.2024.

Why could this be happening?

entry.title: Enterprise Portal BW reports after Netweaver EOL
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I'm currently working on an BW 7.5 on HANA landscape, with plans to upgrade to BW/4HANA in the near future. Considering part of our applications rely on Enterprise Portal to display the reports (web based), what is the usual/recommended path for these reports following the 2027 Netweaver end of life? I know and we do use AfO and SAC in some cases but I'm wondering about a web based solution.

Thanks!

Gabriel

entry.title: Re: Hi experts Which table to refer to view physical qty...
entry.content:
Hi Hiteshrao,

Below are some important tables related to EWM. I hope you find them helpful.

/SCWM/QUAN Quant Attributes
/LIME/NQUANLIME - Stock Quantities
/SCWM/STOCK_IW01WME - Stock Key Material Batch
/SCWM/STOCK_IW03WME - Stock Key Material, Batch, Reference Document
/SCWM/STOCK_IW04WME - Stock Key Material, Sales Order, Project
/SCWM/ORDIM_C Warehouse Tasks Confirmed
/SCWM/ORDIM_CS Serial Numbers for Confirmed Warehouse Task Item
/SCWM/ORDIM_E Exception Codes for Warehouse Task
/SCWM/ORDIM_O Warehouse Tasks Open
/SCWM/ORDIM_OS Serial Number Items for Open Warehouse Tasks
/SCWM/WAVEHDR Wave - Header Information
/SCWM/WAVEITM Wave - Item
/SCDL/DB_DLVH_O Outbound Delivery Header
/SCDL/DB_DLVI_O Outbound Delivery Item
/SCDL/DB_PROCH_O Outbound Delivery Order Header
/SCDL/DB_PROCI_O Outbound Delivery Order Item
/SCDL/DB_PROCH_I Inbound Delivery - Header
/SCDL/DB_PROCI_I Inbound Delivery - Item
/SCDL/DB_REFDOC Reference
/SCDL/DB_REQH Header Inbound Delivery Notification/Outbound Delivery Req.
/SCDL/DB_REQI Item Inbound Delivery Notification / Outbound Del. Request
/SCWM/HUHDR Handling unit header
/HUSSTAT Individual Status for Each Handling
/SCWM Unit/SCWM/HUREF Handling Unit
/SCWM/HUSTOBJ Information about HU Status
/SCWM/GMHUHDR Handling Unit Header of a Goods Movement Log
/SCWM/GMHUITM Handling Unit Item
/SCWM/GMHUSTATHU Status of HUs from Goods Movement Log
/SCWM/GMHUIDENT Additional HU Identificationss for a Goods Movement Log Reference Object
/SCWM/LAGP Storage Bins
/SCWM/LAGPS Bins for Execution Areas and Activit
/SAPAPO/MATKEY Product
/SAPAPO/MATLWH Location Product for Location Warehouse
/SAPAPO/MATLWHST Location Product for Location Whse and Subloc. Storage Type
/SAPAPO/MATTXT Material Descriptions
/SAPAPO/MATEXEC Product: Execution Data
/SAPAPO/MATMAP Mapping Table for Products
/LIME/PI_PAR_BIZ Physical Inventory Document Business Key Parent Object#project #warehouse #ewm
/LIME/PI_LOGITEM Log Table for Item Data of a Physical Inventory Document
/LIME/PI_LOGHIST History Table for Physical Inventory LOG Entries
/LIME/PI_LOGHEAD Log Table for Header Data of a Physical Inventory Document
/LIME/PI_IT_BIZ Physical Inventory Document Business Key Item Object
/LIME/PI_DOC_TB Quantities for Physical Inventory Document Items
/LIME/PI_DOC_SER Serial Numbers for Physical Inventory Document Items
/LIME/PI_DOC_IT Table of Physical Inventory Document Items
/LIME/PI_DOCTYPE Document Types of Physical Inventory (Internal Settings)
/LIME/PI_DCTYTXT Description of Phys. Inventory ProcedureM


Regards
Faraz Rahim
